title: MongoDB Indexing - Complete Guide (Beginner to Advanced)
description: Comprehensive guide on index creation, types, strategies, and performance optimization

indexing_overview.definition: Indexes are data structures that improve query performance by organizing data for faster retrieval
indexing_overview.tradeoff: Indexes speed reads but slow writes (inserts/updates/deletes)

section_1_beginner_single_field.title: BEGINNER: Single Field Indexes
section_1_beginner_single_field.definition: Index on a single field to speed up queries on that field

beginner_examples.title: Index on studentId (primary identifier)
beginner_examples.use_case: Every query by student ID benefits

beginner_examples.title: Index on age (sorting)
beginner_examples.note: Without index, MongoDB sorts in memory

section_1_beginner_single_field.important_note: Every index consumes memory and slows writes; index only fields queried frequently

beginner_examples.title: Drop single index
beginner_examples.effect: Removes index, speeds up writes but slows related queries

beginner_examples.title: Drop all indexes (except _id)
beginner_examples.note: _id index always exists and can't be removed

section_3_intermediate_compound.title: INTERMEDIATE: Compound Indexes (Multi-field)
section_3_intermediate_compound.definition: Index on multiple fields to support queries filtering on multiple criteria
section_3_intermediate_compound.meaning: Field order matters; first field is primary sort

intermediate_examples.title: Index on major + gpa
intermediate_examples.note: Major first (common filter), GPA second (often sorted)
intermediate_examples.use_case: Find high-GPA students in each major

intermediate_examples.title: Index on major + age + gpa
intermediate_examples.benefits: Supports queries on major, age, gpa or any prefix combination
intermediate_examples.note: Prefix usage: can use major, or major+age, or all three

section_3_intermediate_compound.important_note: Order matters; field order should match query pattern

intermediate_examples.title: Unique index (prevents duplicates)
intermediate_examples.effect: Two documents can't have same email
intermediate_examples.use_case: Ensure email uniqueness; combined with index benefit

intermediate_examples.title: Sparse index (ignores null/missing)
intermediate_examples.effect: Includes only documents where field exists
intermediate_examples.benefit: Smaller index, faster queries for 'has scholarship'
intermediate_examples.use_case: Optional field that most documents lack

intermediate_examples.title: Unique + Sparse (combination)
intermediate_examples.query: db.students.createIndex({ phoneNumber: 1 }, { unique: true, sparse: true })
intermediate_examples.effect: Multiple nulls allowed (sparse), but values unique
intermediate_examples.use_case: Optional phone field, unique when present

intermediate_examples.title: TTL index (auto-delete)
intermediate_examples.effect: Documents deleted automatically 1 hour after creation

section_5_intermediate_text.title: INTERMEDIATE: Text Indexes (Full-text Search)
section_5_intermediate_text.definition: Specialized index for searching text content

intermediate_examples.title: Single field text index
intermediate_examples.benefit: Finds documents containing any of the words

intermediate_examples.title: Multiple field text index
intermediate_examples.use_case: Full-text search across name, bio, major

intermediate_examples.title: Text index with weights
intermediate_examples.query: db.students.createIndex({ name: 'text', bio: 'text' }, { weights: { name: 10, bio: 1 } })
intermediate_examples.effect: Name matches ranked higher than bio
intermediate_examples.use_case: Prioritize exact name matches over content

section_6_advanced_partial.title: ADVANCED: Partial Indexes (Conditional)
section_6_advanced_partial.definition: Index only documents matching a filter condition

advanced_examples.title: Index only scholarship students
advanced_examples.benefit: Smaller index, faster queries on scholarship students
advanced_examples.use_case: Queries only on subset of documents

advanced_examples.title: Index with complex condition
advanced_examples.benefit: Only indexes adults who are active or on leave

section_6_advanced_partial.important_note: Partial indexes save space and improve write performance

advanced_examples.title: Building index in background
advanced_examples.note: Allows queries during index build (slower build)

advanced_examples.title: Covering index (eliminates collection scan)
advanced_examples.scenario: Query: db.students.find({ major: 'CS', age: 25 }, { _id: 0, major: 1, age: 1, name: 1 })
advanced_examples.benefit: Returns all data from index without reading collection
advanced_examples.performance: Extremely fast - no disk I/O except index
advanced_examples.use_case: Projection only needed fields in index

intermediate.benefit: Single index supports filter and sort

advanced.benefit: All data from index, zero collection I/O

practice_1_index_for_filters.rule: Index fields used in find() filters
practice_1_index_for_filters.avoid: No index for frequently filtered fields

practice_3_avoid_unnecessary.solution: Review slow queries, create targeted indexes

practice_4_use_partial_for_subsets.rule: Use partial index for document subsets
practice_4_use_partial_for_subsets.good: Index { gpa: 1 } only for hasScholarship: true

practice_5_cover_queries.example: Index { major: 1, gpa: 1 } covers find({ major }, { major, gpa })

mistake_2_wrong_field_order.wrong: Query: find({ gpa: 3.8, major: 'CS' }) vs Index: { major: 1, gpa: 1 }
mistake_2_wrong_field_order.impact: Index still works but not optimally

mistake_3_indexing_low_selectivity.solution: Combine with other fields in compound index

mistake_4_forgetting_id_index.note: _id index always exists and can't be removed
mistake_4_forgetting_id_index.benefit: Automatic, essential for primary key lookups

mistake_5_unique_without_sparse.problem: Can't have two documents with null for optional field
mistake_5_unique_without_sparse.solution: Use { unique: true, sparse: true } for optional fields

monitoring_examples.shows: Size of each index in bytes

beginner: Create index on frequently queried fields

key_concepts: Indexes speed reads but slow writes
Index order matters for compound indexes
Partial indexes save space and improve writes
summary.best_practice: Index frequently queried fields, use compound indexes strategically, monitor usage, drop unused indexes
summary.progression: Beginner: Single field indexes → Intermediate: Compound, Unique, Text → Advanced: Partial, Covering, Optimization
